controls.title: Subject matter
controls.description: Establishes uniform requirements for the security of NIS supporting business processes of financial entities: ICT risk management, incident management/reporting, resilience testing, third-party risk, and information sharing. DORA is maximum harmonisation.

controls.title: Scope
controls.description: Applies to a broad range of financial entities: credit institutions, payment/e-money institutions, investment firms, CASPs, CCPs, CSDs, trading venues, insurers, IORPs, CRAs, crowdfunding providers. Certain SMEs excluded.

controls.title: Definitions
controls.description: Defines key terms: ICT risk, ICT-related incident, major ICT-related incident, cyber threat, critical or important function, ICT third-party service provider, critical ICT TPSP.

controls.title: Proportionality
controls.description: Rules applied proportionately to size, risk profile, nature, scale and complexity. Micro-enterprises and certain entities may apply the simplified framework under Art.16.

controls.title: Governance and organisation
controls.description: Management body bears ultimate responsibility for ICT risk: define risk appetite, approve policies, approve RMF, approve ICT budget, ensure ICT staffing/training, approve major ICT projects, ensure crisis communication, ensure internal audit. At least one board member reports on ICT risk.

controls.title: ICT risk management framework
controls.description: Maintain a robust, well-documented ICT RMF with strategies, policies, procedures, IT protocols, and tools. Review after major incidents and at least annually.

controls.title: ICT systems, protocols and tools
controls.description: ICT systems, protocols, and tools must be appropriate, reliable, technologically resilient, and kept current with security standards (patched, maintained).

controls.title: Identification
controls.description: Identify and classify ICT-supported functions, their ICT and information assets, sources of ICT risk; maintain an updated ICT asset inventory; map interdependencies; identify single points of failure.

controls.title: Protection and prevention
controls.description: Implement controls including information security policies, network segmentation, encryption, third-party access policies, change management, document management, patch and software update policies, and ICT project management policies.

controls.title: Detection
controls.description: Implement appropriate mechanisms to detect anomalous activities including ICT network problems and ICT-related incidents, with multiple layers of control and defined thresholds for alerts.

controls.title: Response and recovery
controls.description: Implement ICT business continuity policy with activation triggers, recovery priorities, interim procedures, BIA for critical functions, RTO/RPO, response and recovery plans. Test continuity plans at least annually.

controls.title: Backup policies and procedures
controls.description: Maintain backup policies specifying frequency, type, location, and scope. Restoration systems separate from primary; tested for restorability aligned to RTO. ICT backup systems may use third-party cloud.

controls.title: Learning and evolving
controls.description: Gather, analyse, and document findings from major incidents and post-incident reviews. Monitor cyber threat intelligence. Integrate threat intelligence into risk assessment. Provide ICT security awareness training; specialist training; board training on ICT risk.

controls.title: Communication
controls.description: Implement crisis communication plans for major ICT incidents or cyber threats. Define internal escalation procedures and external communication to clients, sector counterparts, and public authorities.

controls.title: Further harmonisation
controls.description: ESAs develop RTS on detailed elements of ICT RMF (CDR (EU) 2024/1774). Financial entities must align with the RTS-defined detailed requirements.

controls.title: Simplified ICT risk management framework
controls.description: Small and less complex entities may apply a simplified framework per CDR (EU) 2024/1774 Chapter II covering key RMF elements, simplified testing, and simplified reporting.

controls.title: ICT-related incident management process
controls.description: Establish documented incident management process with roles, criteria, escalation paths. Report major incidents to senior management and board. Communicate with affected clients without undue delay.

controls.title: Classification of incidents and cyber threats
controls.description: Classify incidents based on clients affected, reputational impact, duration, geographic spread, data losses, service criticality, economic impact. ESAs to develop RTS (CDR (EU) 2024/1772).

controls.title: Reporting of major ICT-related incidents
controls.description: Report major incidents to competent authority in three stages: initial within 4 hours of classification as major; intermediate within 72 hours; final within 1 month. Entities may voluntarily notify significant cyber threats.

controls.title: Harmonisation of reporting content, timelines and templates
controls.description: Use ESA-developed templates and content standards (CDR (EU) 2025/301 for content; CIR (EU) 2025/302 for templates).

controls.title: Centralisation of reporting
controls.description: ESAs to assess feasibility of single EU reporting hub. Authorities share incident information with relevant bodies (CSIRTs, ENISA, other regulators).

controls.title: Supervisory feedback
controls.description: Competent authorities may provide feedback after incident reports: impact assessment, threat intelligence, preventive measures.

controls.title: Reporting of payment-related major incidents
controls.description: Applies to credit institutions, payment institutions, e-money institutions. Payment-specific reporting integrated with DORA templates. Supersedes pre-DORA PSD2 Art.96 reporting where DORA major thresholds met.

controls.title: General requirements for resilience testing
controls.description: All financial entities must conduct a testing programme for critical ICT systems at least annually. Tests conducted by independent internal or external parties.

controls.title: Testing of ICT tools and systems
controls.description: Basic testing types: vulnerability assessments and scans, network security assessments, source code reviews, performance tests, end-to-end tests, scenario-based compatibility tests.

controls.title: Advanced testing — Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT)
controls.description: TLPT at least once every 3 years. Live production systems in scope. Covers critical/important functions. Threat intelligence-driven scenarios. Mutual recognition across EU jurisdictions. External testers, no conflict of interest. Applicability criteria in CDR (EU) 2025/1190.

controls.title: Requirements for testers carrying out TLPT
controls.description: Testers must demonstrate methodology capability, technical expertise, hold relevant professional certifications, and have no conflicts of interest. Competent authority maintains list of qualified testers.

controls.title: General principles for ICT third-party risk management
controls.description: Adopt, review, and update ICT third-party risk policy. Maintain Register of Information (RoI) for all ICT service arrangements. Submit RoI annually to competent authority. Pre-contractual due diligence for critical/important arrangements. Ongoing monitoring. Concentration risk assessment. Exit strategies.

controls.title: Preliminary assessment of ICT concentration risk
controls.description: Assess entity-level concentration when entering new arrangements. Assess risk that entire ICT services become unavailable. Required prior to arrangements for critical functions.

controls.title: Key contractual provisions
controls.description: Mandatory provisions for critical/important function contracts: service description, data locations, data protection, availability/security, audit and access rights, termination rights, reporting, data portability, sub-contracting. Lighter provisions for non-critical. RTS in CDR (EU) 2024/1773, CDR (EU) 2025/532.

controls.title: Designation of critical ICT third-party service providers
controls.description: ESAs designate critical ICT TPSPs based on CDR (EU) 2024/1502 criteria. Non-EU CTPSPs must designate EU legal representative.

controls.title: Structure of the oversight framework
controls.description: Lead Overseer (EBA/ESMA/EIOPA) designated per CTPSP. Joint Oversight Network (JON). Joint Examination Teams (JETs) per CDR (EU) 2025/420.
evidence_types: Awareness of Lead Overseer for used CTPSPs

controls.title: Information requests
controls.description: Lead Overseer may require CTPSPs to provide all relevant information and documentation.

controls.title: General investigations
controls.description: Lead Overseer may conduct general investigations including interviews and document reviews.

controls.title: Inspections
controls.description: Lead Overseer may conduct on-site inspections; CTPSPs must cooperate.

controls.title: Oversight recommendations
controls.description: Lead Overseer issues recommendations on significant findings; CTPSP has 6 weeks to respond.

controls.title: Follow-up of oversight recommendations
controls.description: If CTPSP fails to implement recommendations: escalated follow-up; public disclosure of non-compliance possible.

controls.title: Oversight fees
controls.description: Annual oversight fees for CTPSPs per CDR (EU) 2024/1505.

controls.title: Rights of defence
controls.description: CTPSPs have right to be heard before formal findings are issued; access to documents.

controls.title: Cooperation between competent authorities
controls.description: Competent authorities of financial entities that use a CTPSP exchange relevant information with the Lead Overseer — supervisory findings, complaints, material changes. Financial entities cooperate with their own competent authority on CTPSP arrangements.

controls.title: Cooperation between competent authorities and Lead Overseers
controls.description: Exchange of information; coordination mechanisms; JON information sharing. RTS on harmonisation (CDR (EU) 2025/295).

controls.title: Liability of financial entities
controls.description: Designation of a CTPSP does not affect the financial entity's own responsibility for DORA compliance. Entities cannot outsource regulatory obligations to a CTPSP.

controls.title: Oversight fees collection
controls.description: Lead Overseer collects fees; methodology per CDR (EU) 2024/1505.

controls.title: International cooperation
controls.description: ESAs may conclude administrative arrangements with third-country regulatory authorities on information exchange for oversight of CTPSPs with global footprint.
evidence_types: Awareness of international arrangements affecting used CTPSPs

controls.title: Cyber threat intelligence sharing arrangements
controls.description: Financial entities may voluntarily participate in cyber threat intelligence sharing arrangements with other financial entities. Must protect confidential information and comply with competition rules.

controls.title: Competent authorities
controls.description: Each Member State designates competent authorities for each financial entity type. Multiple authorities may be designated (e.g., ECB for SSM banks + national NCA).

controls.title: Cooperation with NIS2 structures
controls.description: Competent authorities under DORA cooperate with bodies established under NIS2, including information sharing with national CSIRTs and ENISA.

controls.title: Cooperation between competent authorities
controls.description: Mutual assistance; supervisory convergence across Member States.

controls.title: Cross-sectoral exercises
controls.description: Competent authorities may participate in cross-sectoral crisis simulations with other regulatory bodies and public authorities.

controls.title: Administrative penalties and remedial measures
controls.description: Competent authorities have supervisory powers: orders to cease non-compliant conduct, administrative penalties, suspension of ICT service arrangements posing systemic risk.

controls.title: Exercise of penalty power
controls.description: Member States determine effective, proportionate, and dissuasive penalties. Penalties can apply to both legal entities and natural persons in management.

controls.title: Criminal penalties
controls.description: Member States may provide for criminal penalties for DORA violations.

controls.title: Notification and publication of penalties
controls.description: Competent authorities publish information on penalties and measures imposed (naming and shaming regime).

controls.title: EBA Lead for banking
controls.description: EBA acts as Lead Overseer for banking-sector CTPSPs; coordinates with ESMA and EIOPA.
evidence_types: Awareness of EBA role for banking CTPSPs

controls.title: ESMA Lead for capital markets
controls.description: ESMA acts as Lead Overseer for capital markets CTPSPs.
evidence_types: Awareness of ESMA role for capital markets CTPSPs

controls.title: EIOPA Lead for insurance and pensions
controls.description: EIOPA acts as Lead Overseer for insurance, reinsurance, and pension fund CTPSPs.
evidence_types: Awareness of EIOPA role for insurance/pensions CTPSPs

controls.title: Exercise of the delegation
controls.description: Empowers Commission to adopt delegated regulations (the RTS/ITS). Delegation runs for 5 years from 17 January 2025, renewable.

controls.title: Transitional provisions
controls.description: Procedures for handling pending supervisory cases at the time of DORA application.

controls.title: Amendments to MiCA
controls.description: DORA imposes ICT risk obligations on CASPs as defined in MiCA; Art.59 makes consequential amendments to align MiCA with DORA.

controls.title: Amendments to EMIR
controls.description: Consequential amendments for CCPs and trade repositories.

controls.title: Amendments to MiFIR
controls.description: Consequential amendments for trading venues and data reporting service providers.

controls.title: Amendments to AIFMD
controls.description: Consequential amendments for AIFMs.

controls.title: Entry into force
controls.description: DORA entered into force on 16 January 2023 (20 days after OJ publication on 27 December 2022).

controls.title: Application
controls.description: DORA applies from 17 January 2025 — 24 months after entry into force.
